title: Environmental Design
author: From: Atomic Habits
content: You do not rise to the level of your goals; you fall to the level of your systems. Design your environment to make good habits the path of least resistance.
action: Reorganize physical workspace to prioritize primary objectives.

title: Decision Hygiene
author: From: How to Decide
content: Good decisions require good data. Before making a choice, identify what data you are missing that would change your mind.
action: Define the specific data threshold that would trigger a decision reversal.

title: The Planning Fallacy
author: From: Thinking, Fast and Slow
content: We consistently underestimate how long tasks will take. Always add a 50% buffer to your best-case estimate.
action: Apply a 1.5x multiplier to all upcoming project time estimates.

title: Negative Space
author: From: The Design of Everyday Things
content: What you *don't* do is just as important as what you do. Create space for reflection by saying 'no' to non-essential inputs.
action: Eliminate one redundant commitment to preserve cognitive bandwidth.

title: Cognitive Load Management
author: From: Deep Work
content: Your brain has a limited budget for deep thought. Protect this budget by ruthlessly eliminating shallow, reactive work.
action: Enforce a 'No-Email' block during the first two hours of the workday.
